untranslatable_reason: Signal requires calculation of correlation coefficient between lagged returns and future returns with statistical significance testing (p-value < 0.05). This is a dynamic statistical test that cannot be expressed using only the allowed indicators and comparison operators. The correlation calculation and p-value threshold are computational operations beyond the scope of the rule engine's indicator-based logic. Lookback and holding periods are parameterized (25/60/120 days and 1/5/10/25/60/120/250 days respectively) requiring external computation.
translation_notes: This momentum/mean-reversion signal is fundamentally a parametric statistical test (Pearson correlation with significance testing) rather than a technical indicator comparison. The rule engine would need to support lag-correlation functions and hypothesis testing to properly implement this strategy. A simplified proxy using returns direction has been shown above, but this does not capture the actual signal logic of identifying correlation significance across multiple timeframes.
